spec.reasoning: Two safe perturbation strategies are applied. (1) Title row insert: A title row ('Transaction Records') is inserted at row 1 of Sheet1, pushing the header down to row 2 and data to rows 3-5. This tests whether the model hardcodes row 1 as the header row. The instruction references 'Column B' for output (B1:B4 becomes B2:B5 in the new layout), but since the instruction says 'output the answer in Column B' generically and the answer position will be adjusted accordingly, this is a safe surface change. (2) Distractor column: A plausible but irrelevant column 'Transaction Notes' is added after the last column. This tests whether the model gets confused by extra columns. The header name in column A is extremely long and unique — it is essentially a data value serving as a header — so renaming it would risk changing the data content, which is unsafe. No column or sheet renames are applied to keep data integrity intact.
